_meta.unit: Other-houses queue, house 9 - Fendi lexicon: models (with debut year + designer) + materials/signature-motifs (FF Zucca logo + Selleria leather line) + named colors/signatures

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). DESIGNER attribution is the load-bearing fact: the BAGS are Silvia Venturini Fendi's (Baguette 1997, Spy 2005, Peekaboo ~2008/2009); Karl Lagerfeld led FUR + RTW 1965-2019 and designed the LOGOS (FF Zucca, Karligraphy) not the bag silhouettes; Kim Jones led womenswear/couture 2020-2024 (First 2021, Fendigraphy 2022); Maria Grazia Chiuri took over womenswear/couture 2025 (debut Feb 2026).
types.material: signature motif / leather / logo. TWO-signature DNA: the FF 'Zucca' double-F logo (Lagerfeld 1965/66, 'Fun Fur') incl. Zucchino (smaller FF) + FF 1974 (modern embossed) + Karligraphy (1981); AND the Selleria hand-saddle-stitched leather line ('saddlery' in Italian, technique to the family 1925, modern handbag 1960s, on Cuoio Romano leather). Plus Pequin stripe, fur/shearling, exotics.
types.color: named color / signature. KEY HONEST FINDING: Fendi does NOT publish a per-season color-name lexicon (the Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine pattern, NOT Hermes/Bottega). Colors are plain descriptors (Brown, Black, Camelia, Tobacco); naming weight sits on the MODEL + the LOGO/MATERIAL treatment (Zucca canvas vs Selleria leather vs FF 1974), not the color. official_or_descriptive marks each.

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. ATTRIBUTION: the bags are SILVIA VENTURINI FENDI's (Baguette 1997, Spy 2005, Peekaboo ~2008/2009) - do NOT over-credit Lagerfeld, who led FUR+RTW 1965-2019 and designed the LOGOS only. Kim Jones led womenswear/couture 2020-2024 (First 2021, Fendigraphy 2022). 2025-2026 UPDATE (post-dates the brief): Silvia moved to Honorary President Oct 1 2025 (stays on accessories/menswear); Maria Grazia Chiuri became Creative Director of Womenswear+Couture, debut Feb 2026. CULTURAL: the Baguette is the It-bag origin via Sex and the City ('It's not a bag, it's a Baguette!', 2000 episode), discontinued in the 2000s, revived 2019, 25th-anniv NYFW Sept 2022, 1000+ variations. COLORS: Fendi does NOT name its per-season colors (same finding as Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine) - logged honestly, NOT invented. Kan I/Kan U + Touch did NOT surface with clean debut years - logged low/unsourced, NOT fabricated; 'C'mon' did not surface at all and is omitted not invented. fendi.com NOT scraped wholesale (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior/Gucci/BV/YSL/Celine); fendi.com product copy surfaced as search snippets. No Christie's/Sotheby's Fendi page surfaced; sources were Fashionphile Academy (Herstory + Logos WORKHORSES), PurseBop (Kim Jones Fall 2021 WORKHORSE), Luxury London (buyer's guide WORKHORSE), W Magazine (Selleria WORKHORSE), PurseBlog, Spotted Fashion.
conflicts: FF Zucca date: Fashionphile/PurseBop say 1965; Luxury London says 1966 - logged 1965 high w/ 1966 noted
Peekaboo debut: Fashionphile+Harper's say 'launched 2008' (orig. 'Hide and Seek'); Luxury London says SS2009 runway - logged ~2008/2009
Selleria: technique came to the family 1925 (W Magazine); the modern Selleria HANDBAG introduced by Adele Fendi in the 1960s (L'Officiel) - both captured, not a conflict

coverage.models: 22 model rows (Baguette+Nano/Mini, Mamma Baguette, Peekaboo+ISeeU+Mini/Petite, Spy, Sunshine, Mon Tresor, First, Fendigraphy, By the Way, 2Jours, Dotcom, Roma, Kan I/Kan U low, Touch low, Way + bonus Croissant/Roll/Origami/Fendessence); designer attributed on every row; debut years sourced for Baguette/Mamma/Peekaboo ISeeU/Spy/Mon Tresor/First/Fendigraphy/By the Way/Dotcom/Origami
coverage.materials: 9 motifs/leathers/logos (FF Zucca + Zucchino + FF 1974 + Karligraphy as the logo DNA; Selleria + Cuoio Romano as the leather DNA; Pequin stripe, fur/shearling, exotics)
coverage.colors: 5 plain-descriptor rows (Brown/Camelia/Black/Tobacco/Spring Turquoise) + an explicit 'no per-season color lexicon exists' finding. NO invented seasonal color names.

name: Baguette
status: heritage icon; discontinued in the 2000s, revived 2019, 25th-anniv NYFW Sept 2022
note: THE original 'It bag': short shoulder bag, longer than tall, tucked under the arm like a loaf of bread; FF flap clasp; 1000+ variations. Sex and the City fame (2000 episode, 'It's not a bag, it's a Baguette!'). Double-sourced: Fashionphile (1997, SATC) + Luxury London (born 1997, revived 2019, 25th anniv 2022, 1000+ variations) + Harper's Bazaar.

name: Baguette Nano / Mini
note: Miniaturized Baguette: Nano = micro/charm size, Mini = small crossbody; central to the modern revival. Year of the mini/nano sizes not separately sourced (the parent Baguette is 1997).

name: Mamma Baguette
note: Deconstructed/relaxed Baguette reissue in soft nappa leather with a DRAWSTRING closure; 3 sizes; FF logo buckle. Revives the 1990s slouchy 'Mamma' Baguette shape. Double-sourced: Luxury London (new for 2025, nappa + drawstring, 3 sizes) + official Fendi TikTok (Mamma Baguette reimagined for #FendiSS25). Spelled both Mama and Mamma.

name: Peekaboo
note: Silvia's quiet-luxury answer to logomania: trapezium top-handle with a twist lock on EACH side, worn open to reveal a contrasting interior lining (hence peek-a-boo); originally called 'Hide and Seek'. DEBUT CONFLICT FLAGGED: Fashionphile + Harper's say 'launched 2008'; Luxury London says SS2009 runway debut - logged ~2008/2009. Double-sourced (Fashionphile + Luxury London + Harper's).

name: Peekaboo ISeeU
note: More structured, ACCORDION-style Peekaboo with a wide opening so you can 'see U' the interior; East-West (horizontal) + rectangular variants exist. Launched Fall 2020. Sourced: PurseBop (Peekaboo ISeeU launched Fall 2020, accordion, East-West).

name: Peekaboo Mini / Petite
note: Downsized Peekaboo (Mini, and the newer Peekaboo ISeeU Petite); centenary-era smaller-leather-goods addition (~2024-2025). Sourced: W Magazine (Peekaboo Petite as new smaller Selleria style, 2024) + fendi.com (Peekaboo ISeeU Petite).

name: Spy
status: heritage (largely retired; revived in Chiuri Feb 2026 debut)
note: Slouchy boho hobo with a braided top handle + hidden compartments (incl. a lipstick slot in the handle); defined the mid-2000s boho-chic era; inspired by the curved Fendi Croissant. Sourced: Fashionphile Herstory (Spy 2005).

name: Sunshine Shopper (Sunshine tote)
note: Open structured shopper tote with cut-out top handles, often 'FENDI ROMA' lettering on the front. Reimagined under Kim Jones (Fall 2021); ORIGINAL debut year not cleanly sourced this run (flagged null, not invented). Sourced: PurseBop (Sunshine Shopper Fall 2021) + Rebag/IG 2025 + PurseBlog.

name: Mon Tresor (Mini Bucket)
note: Compact DRAWSTRING bucket bag with a soft knotted drawstring + metal ends; runs in FF-embossed calfskin + Zucca canvas. Runway look Women's Fall 2018; line current. Double-sourced: fendi.com (Mon Tresor bucket, knotted drawstring, FF-embossed calfskin) + Les Facons (Mon Tresor bucket, Women's Fall 2018 catwalk).

name: First / Fendi First
note: Kim Jones's debut bag: slouchy coin-purse pouch with a large F-SHAPED CLASP (in lieu of a ball clasp); runs as pouch/clutch/chain bag; Nano/Small/Medium. Fall 2021 (runway March 4, 2021). Double-sourced: PurseBop (Fendi First, Kim Jones debut RTW Fall 2021, F-shaped clasp) + PurseBlog (Fendi First, 2021 Winter Capsule).

name: Fendigraphy
note: Logo-forward HOBO / crescent-moon shape with large 'Fendi' lettering across the curved base in gold metal; removable strap; Nano/Small/Medium. Sourced: Luxury London (Fendigraphy by Kim Jones, introduced 2022, hobo crescent, Fendi lettering on the base).

name: By the Way
note: Boston-style DOUBLE-ZIP top-handle bag with removable strap; immediate 2014 hit; 10th-anniversary Selleria revamp for AW24; mini to full-size. Sourced: Luxury London (By the Way launched 2014, 10th-anniv Selleria revamp AW24) + IG (By The Way Mini, zip fastening).

name: 2Jours (Deux Jours)
note: Structured trapezoidal tote with COLLAPSIBLE side panels (open = roomy, closed = sleek); side panels in pony hair/suede; a 2010s workhorse. Debut year not cleanly sourced this run (~2012 per beat, flagged null not invented). Sourced: Spotted Fashion (2Jours reference guide, side panels pony hair/suede).

name: Dotcom (Dot.Com)
note: Structured top-handle bag with a rounded body; first seen in the Resort 2016 lookbook, shown SS2016. Sourced: Spotted Fashion (Dot.Com top-handle, first seen Resort 2016 lookbook, SS2016 runway).

name: Roma / FENDI ROMA signature
note: 'FENDI ROMA' / 'Roma' is the house heritage signature (founded Rome 1925), appearing across logo totes + the Mamma Baguette - not a single bag model. Sourced: PurseBop (Sunshine with FENDI ROMA front) + W Magazine (Fendi's Roman roots, 1925).

name: Kan I / Kan U
note: Structured saddle/flap shoulder bag with a circular FF-logo turn-lock on the flap; Kan U is the later softer evolution. BRIEFED model; identity is beat knowledge, clean debut year did NOT surface this run (~Kan I 2016, Kan U 2018 per beat, UNCONFIRMED). Logged low/unsourced, NOT invented. Queue: fendi.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum.

name: Touch / Fendi Touch
note: Soft top-handle shoulder bag with twin rolled handles; a Kim-Jones-era softer everyday shape. BRIEFED model; clean debut year did NOT surface this run (~2021 per beat, UNCONFIRMED). Logged low/unsourced, NOT invented. Queue: fendi.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum.

name: Way / Fendi Way tote
note: Large logo tote with narrow top handles + a metal double-F just below the handles; leather-corner covers. Reimagined Fall 2021 (debut year of the original Way tote not separately sourced). Sourced: PurseBop (extra-large Fendi Way tote, narrow handles, metal FF, Fall 2021).

name: Croissant
note: Curved crescent-shaped under-the-arm bag that preceded + inspired the Spy. Pre-2005. Sourced: Fashionphile Herstory (Fendi Croissant, crescent shape, inspired the Spy).

name: Roll bag
note: Reversible cylindrical bag created alongside the Baguette in 1997, later remade in the Pequin stripe. Sourced: official Fendi Facebook (the Roll bag, created by Silvia Venturini Fendi in 1997 alongside the Baguette, reimagined reversible with Pequin).

name: Origami
note: Multi-functional bag that folds into a cubic bucket, a trapezoidal shopper, or a flower-like shape (named after Japanese paper-folding). Sourced: Luxury London (Origami launched end of 2023 by Silvia Venturini Fendi, folds into multiple shapes).

name: Fendessence (Mini Fendessence hobo)
note: Small hobo in Selleria; a centenary-era smaller leather good (~2024). Sourced: W Magazine (Mini Fendessence hobo as a new Selleria style, 2024). Single-source, low.

name: FF 'Zucca' logo (the double-F)
note: THE Fendi signature: interlocking double-F monogram, 'FF' = 'Fun Fur(s)', sketched by Lagerfeld; the 'inverted Zucca' print that defined noughties logomania. DATE CONFLICT FLAGGED: Fashionphile/PurseBop say 1965; Luxury London says 1966 - logged 1965 high. Sourced: Fashionphile Logos + PurseBop.

name: Zucchino (mini FF)
note: SMALLER-scale version of the FF logo, a jacquard fabric pattern (the 'mini Zucca'); out of production (exact discontinuation year uncertain); distinct from the larger Zucca. Sourced: Fashionphile Logos (Zucchino, mini FF, introduced 1965, jacquard, no longer produced).

name: FF 1974 (modern embossed double-F)
note: Modern reinterpretation: the inverted Zucca double-F EMBOSSED in 3D on supple lambskin (or 'fabric jacquard FF 1974'); the contemporary FF treatment. Sourced: Fashionphile Logos (FF 1974, embossed double-F, inverted Zucca in 3D on lambskin).

name: Karligraphy
note: Lagerfeld's calligraphic FF emblem blending his own hand with the FF mark; first on cabochon buttons + intarsia fur, now on bags (e.g. a Fendi First variant). Double-sourced: Fashionphile Logos (Karligraphy, Lagerfeld 1981) + PurseBop (FF Karligraphy monogram Lagerfeld designed 1981).

name: Selleria (the leather line)
maps_to: Selleria bags (Peekaboo, Baguette, By the Way Selleria)
note: Fendi's hand-saddle-stitched leather line: 'Selleria' = 'saddlery' in Italian, a tone-on-tone top-stitching technique from Rome's master saddlers, taught to the Fendis 1925; hand-sewn on Cuoio Romano leather; each bag carries a sterling-silver tag engraved with a serial + the year 1925; visible contrast saddle stitching. NUANCE (not a conflict): technique 1925 (W Magazine); modern Selleria HANDBAG introduced by Adele Fendi in the 1960s (L'Officiel). Double-sourced.

name: Cuoio Romano leather
note: The buttery, nubby-textured Roman leather Fendi uses for Selleria pieces; the signature Selleria hide. Sourced: W Magazine (Cuoio Romano leathers, buttery + nubby-textured, the Selleria base).

name: Pequin stripe
note: A Fendi heritage STRIPED motif (tonal brown/beige vertical stripes), used on the Roll bag + accessories; a quieter logo alternative to the Zucca. Sourced: official Fendi Facebook (Roll bag reimagined with Pequin) + beat knowledge.

name: Touch of Fur / fur + shearling trims
note: Fendi's founding craft is FUR (house began 1925 as a fur+leather atelier; Lagerfeld modernized the fur dept from 1965, hence FF = 'Fun Fur'); fur trims, shearling, 'teddy bear' shearling recur on bags. Double-sourced: Fashionphile Herstory + PurseBop (teddy bear shearling First + Peekaboo, fur trims Fall 2021).

name: Exotic skins (crocodile / ostrich / python)
note: Fendi is one of few houses still using crocodile, ostrich, python/snakeskin on bags (esp. Baguette + Peekaboo). Sourced: Luxury London (Baguette in crocodile/ostrich/calfskin/lambskin/fur) + PurseBop (python-print First, snakeskin).

name: Brown
maps_to: Mon Tresor / By the Way / calfskin bags
note: The default Mon Tresor / By the Way / calfskin neutral, often with the FF motif hand-painted in black. Sourced: fendi.com (Small Mon Tresor, brown calfskin embossed + hand-painted FF in black; By The Way Mini brown leather). PLAIN descriptor, not a season name.

name: Camelia (listed 'White')
note: A pale camelia-toned calfskin (off-white/cream); about as close as Fendi gets to a 'named' color, and it is a plain shade descriptor listed under 'White', not a season name. Sourced: fendi.com (Mon Tresor, camelia-colored calfskin, listed 'White').

name: Black
note: Core structured-bag color (Peekaboo, First, Baguette); the minimalist default. Sourced: PurseBop (Fendi First in black) + eBay (Sunshine Shopper black). PLAIN descriptor.

name: Tobacco
note: A warm brown shown on the Zucca Baguette; a shade descriptor on the logo canvas. Sourced: Fashionphile Logos (Fendi Zucca Baguette in the color Tobacco). PLAIN descriptor.

name: Spring Turquoise
note: A bright turquoise on the FF 1974 embossed Baguette; a seasonal SHADE descriptor, not a poetic house name. Sourced: Fashionphile Logos (Nappa FF 1974 Embossed Baguette in Spring Turquoise). PLAIN descriptor, single-source, low.

name: NO per-season color-name lexicon (honest finding)
note: KEY HONEST FINDING: Fendi does NOT name its per-season colors (the Dior/Gucci/YSL/Celine pattern, NOT Hermes/Bottega). Naming weight is on the LOGO canvas (FF Zucca/Zucchino/FF 1974) + the LEATHER line (Selleria), with colors as plain descriptors (Brown, Black, Camelia). fendi.com color field is a flat shade name (e.g. 'White' for camelia calfskin), never a season-coded poetic name. Do NOT log invented Fendi season colors.
